raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus on surface-level writing polish only, or on substantive section-level argument work including framing, logic, evidence alignment, and structure?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to help with a research section can centrally involve improving the thesis, evaluation object, and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The section contains an argument from benchmark construction and accuracy results to user-intent alignment, so checking the premise-to-conclusion chain is centrally implicated.","R1.3":"The section makes claims about interpretation alignment and empirical superiority that need to match the reported benchmark and metrics.","R1.5":"Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central expected responsibilities.","R1.6":"A general request for help with a section commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Any help should disclose uncertainty about author intent and unavailable surrounding paper context.","RX.2":"The projection must account for avoiding inflated claims such as equating majority-vote labels with individual user intent.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within helping with the provided section rather than executing broader paper revisions or external audits.","RX.4":"The section concerns alignment and user intent, so avoiding downstream misleading claims or misuse is always active.","RX.5":"Any projected responsibility should preserve traceability to the provided artifact and distinguish reported results from interpretation."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus on surface-level writing polish only, or on substantive section-level argument work including framing, logic, evidence alignment, and structure?

rationale.R1.1: The request to help with a research section can centrally involve improving the thesis, evaluation object, and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The section contains an argument from benchmark construction and accuracy results to user-intent alignment, so checking the premise-to-conclusion chain is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes claims about interpretation alignment and empirical superiority that need to match the reported benchmark and metrics.
rationale.R1.5: Because the delegation targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.R1.6: A general request for help with a section commonly includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any help should disclose uncertainty about author intent and unavailable surrounding paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must account for avoiding inflated claims such as equating majority-vote labels with individual user intent.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within helping with the provided section rather than executing broader paper revisions or external audits.
rationale.RX.4: The section concerns alignment and user intent, so avoiding downstream misleading claims or misuse is always active.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should preserve traceability to the provided artifact and distinguish reported results from interpretation.
